column1: # C vs Rust golden image mapping for bit-match verification
# Only list pairs where C and Rust perform the SAME operation on the SAME input.
# Exclude tiled displays, gplot outputs, and format-incompatible comparisons.
# Note: entries 0-2 are 1bpp — affected by PNG polarity bug (see ccthin1 note)
# KNOWN: JPEG codec diff (libjpeg vs jpeg-encoder). Algorithm verified correct
# Note: C check 5 is the clipped region itself (not thinned), Rust skips it
# KNOWN: 1bpp PNG polarity bug — Rust PNG I/O doesn't invert 1bpp pixels.
# C: Pix fg=1 → PNG 0=black (inverted). Rust: Pix fg=1 → PNG 1 (no inversion).
# Algorithm is correct (bit-inversion gives 100% match). Fix tracked in I/O module.
# KNOWN: same 1bpp PNG polarity bug as ccthin1 (see above).
# Note: C test has NO regTestWritePixAndCheck (values only).
# Verified via scripts/verify_binmorph.c: all 4 pairs IDENTICAL (0 pixel diff).
# C index is synthetic (not from reg output).
# C: feyn-fract.tif → separable dilate(11,1)+(1,7), direct dilate(11,7), dilate(21,1)
# Note: C test has NO regTestWritePixAndCheck (values only).
# Verified via scripts/verify_binmorph.c: all 3 pairs IDENTICAL (0 pixel diff).
# Also verified C separability: sep(11,1)+(1,7) == dir(11,7).
# C: feyn-fract.tif → pixHMT with thinning SELs (selaMakeThinSets)
# Note: C test has NO regTestWritePixAndCheck (compare_pix only).
# Verified via scripts/verify_fhmtauto.c: all 8 pairs IDENTICAL (0 pixel diff).
# Set8cc1 = selaMakeThinSets(5,0): sel_8_2, sel_8_3, sel_8_5, sel_8_6
# Note: C test has NO regTestWritePixAndCheck (compare_pix only).
# KNOWN: JPEG codec diff (libjpeg vs jpeg-encoder), quality=75.
